# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  def create
    uploaded_file = params[:image]
    target_format = params[:format]
    target_size = params[:size]

    # Save the uploaded file to a temporary location
    temp_file_path = Rails.root.join('tmp', uploaded_file.original_filename)
    File.open(temp_file_path, 'wb') do |file|
      file.write(uploaded_file.read)
    end

    # Generate the output file path
    output_file_path = Rails.root.join('tmp', "#{File.basename(uploaded_file.original_filename, '.*')}.#{target_format}")

    # Call ImageMagick convert command
    if target_size.present?
      system("convert #{temp_file_path} -resize #{target_size} #{output_file_path}")
    else
      system("convert #{temp_file_path} #{output_file_path}")
    end

    # Send the converted file as a response
    send_file output_file_path, type: "image/#{target_format}", disposition: 'attachment'
  ensure
    # Clean up temporary files
    File.delete(temp_file_path) if File.exist?(temp_file_path)
    File.delete(output_file_path) if File.exist?(output_file_path)
  end
end
